name: Byzantine Empire (Palaiologan banner, c.1261–1453)
description: A yellow (gold) field charged with a black double-headed eagle facing forward, often shown crowned and holding a sword and orb in its talons.
history: The double-headed eagle on gold became closely associated with the Palaiologos dynasty (1261–1453), the last imperial house of the Byzantine Empire. The eagle's two heads symbolised the emperor's claim to authority over both East and West (the legacy of Old and New Rome). The motif was adopted by many subsequent Orthodox states (Russia, Serbia, the Holy Roman Empire and others).
